UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile in the PortfolioForge application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

linkedInUrl.description: URL to the user's LinkedIn profile.

githubUrl.description: URL to the user's GitHub profile.

customDomain.description: The custom domain connected to the user's portfolio, if any.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project showcased in the user's portfolio, sourced from GitHub or Firebase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

projectUrl.description: URL to the live project or GitHub repository.

Theme.title: Theme
Theme.description: Represents a theme available for portfolio customization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Theme entity.

previewImageUrl.description: URL to a preview image of the theme.

cssUrl.description: URL to the theme's CSS file.

Portfolio.title: Portfolio
Portfolio.description: Represents a user's portfolio, linking user profile and selected theme.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Portfolio entity.

customizations.description: JSON string storing custom theme customizations.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Only the user can read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (same as request.auth.uid).

definition.description: Stores projects associated with a specific user. Only the user can read/write their own projects.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (same as request.auth.uid).

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project.

definition.description: Stores the portfolio of a user. Only the user can read/write their own portfolio.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (same as request.auth.uid).

definition.description: Stores themes available for portfolios. Publicly readable.

params.name: themeId
params.description: The unique ID of the theme.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to address the 'auth/operation-not-allowed' error, which typically indicates that the necessary authentication methods (e.g., email/password sign-in) are not enabled in the Firebase console. While the data structure itself doesn't directly fix this, the design promotes secure data access once the authentication methods are enabled.

The structure is based on path-based ownership for user profiles, projects, and portfolios, promoting authorization independence. Specifically:

*   **User Profiles:** Stored under `/users/{userId}/profile`. Access is restricted to the authenticated user (request.auth.uid == userId).
*   **Projects:**  Each user's projects are stored under `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}`. This structure inherently provides ownership and simplifies security rules.
*   **Portfolios:**  Each user's portfolio is stored under `/users/{userId}/portfolio`.  This structure provides ownership and simplifies security rules. Portfolio documents include the `themeId`.
*   **Themes:** Themes are stored in a top-level `/themes/{themeId}` collection and are publicly readable.

This structure facilitates secure `list` operations (QAPs) because each collection is homogeneous in terms of security requirements.  For example, listing projects under `/users/{userId}/projects` only returns projects owned by that user. The structure avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies by directly associating data with the user's ID in the path, making security rules easier to write and debug.

Authorization Independence is achieved as no collection relies on parent data for security. Each document's access is determined solely by the `request.auth.uid` and the document's location in the path.
